mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment rate above 5.5% over 3 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production index below 110.5 over 2 months signals a weakening demand for goods and services that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production index are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above 1 million units over 3 months signals an increase in supply of new homes that reduces housing prices and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real GDP growth below 2% over 6 months signals a weakening economy that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining real GDP growth are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in payroll employment above 140,000 over 1 month signals a strengthening labor market that increases consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising payroll employment are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real disposable personal income above 4% over 3 months signals an increase in consumer spending that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising real disposable personal income are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production index below 105 over 2 months signals a weakening demand for goods and services that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production index are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
